gate_rows.name: receipt provenance root
required_preconditions: monitor-owned receipt minting remains external to Linux
Linux-visible shadows are derived cache records only
opaque types do not define receipt layout or authority
forbidden_fallbacks: Linux opaque id as monitor receipt
Linux cached shadow as response authority
gate_rows.patch_precondition: concrete receipt carrier proposal names monitor-owned minting and Linux-only shadow fields separately

gate_rows.name: hot-path bounded consumption
required_preconditions: hot-path operation is a bounded generation, epoch, or shadow validity comparison
request copy and schema negotiation are not performed in hot path
no sleeping, allocation, tracefs dependency, or monitor receipt minting is introduced in pick/wake/enqueue/switch paths
forbidden_fallbacks: context switch completion as monitor activation proof
gate_rows.patch_precondition: hot-path proposal has bounded-operation proof and fallback behavior model before code review

gate_rows.name: policy and lifecycle separation
required_preconditions: policy/lifecycle paths list request fields they shape
policy/lifecycle paths list receipt fields they cannot decide
fork, exec, and exit identity propagation is modeled separately from receipt authority
forbidden_fallbacks: cpuset intersection as monitor allowed CPU receipt
task_struct duplication as inherited monitor receipt
exec as Domain switch or receipt refresh
gate_rows.patch_precondition: request-shaping path has explicit monitor handoff and no schema-acceptance shortcut

gate_rows.name: generic async exclusion
required_preconditions: generic workqueue and io_uring worker paths are excluded by default
Domain-originated async work requires a separately modeled typed carrier
service authority and caller frozen authority intersection is modeled before allowing async consumption
forbidden_fallbacks: work_struct pending state as caller-specific receipt storage
worker task identity as caller Domain authority
io-wq loop as preserving direct-call receipt authority
gate_rows.patch_precondition: no generic async receipt consumer hook unless a typed carrier model and source map exist

gate_rows.name: future gap preservation
required_preconditions: future gaps remain blockers until their own concrete gate exists
trace-only and test-only rows remain side gates
gap classification does not resolve implementation obligations
forbidden_fallbacks: future gap row as implemented helper
gate_rows.patch_precondition: each converted gap row must name a separate source anchor, monitor responsibility, and validation class

gate_rows.name: revoke and shadow invalidation
required_preconditions: stale Linux shadow invalidation is ordered after monitor revoke receipt
in-flight response handling is modeled before reuse
timeout and retry do not refresh authority
forbidden_fallbacks: cached status after revoke as valid response
timeout refresh as renewed response handle
gate_rows.patch_precondition: revoke ordering model covers stale shadow, in-flight response, timeout, retry, and re-entry

gate_rows.name: evidence class split
required_preconditions: source-only, compile-only, trace-only, model, monitor-backed runtime, and production protection evidence are separately labeled
validation plan names which class each run belongs to
non-claims remain attached to every pre-patch artifact
forbidden_fallbacks: source-map ok row as semantic validation
gate_rows.patch_precondition: patch proposal includes evidence-class table and explicit non-claims

required_before_patch: DCPGATE-001 through DCPGATE-007 satisfied or carried as explicit blockers
symbol/pattern source anchors for every current Linux surface
bounded-operation statement for any scheduler hot-path touch
request-shaping versus receipt-authority table for policy/lifecycle paths
typed-carrier model reference for any Domain-originated async path
evidence-class table with non-claims for ABI, tracepoints, runtime coverage, monitor verification, behavior change, and production protection
